title: Copying saved docker-tar to Registry with Same Sha value as Source Registry
body:
Hi,

Here is my use case:

I have a source Registry where I have say n number of images. I have tar files as well of those images. I want to copy the tar files of these images to Dest Registry however when I do so I see that SHA values on Source and Dest Registries are different. I want them same as my helm charts refer to images using SHA. I cannot do Registry->Registry copy for some internal reasons, I mandatorily have to use tar files.

Is this use case supported by Skopeo? If yes, how can I achieve same SHA as of Source Registry? Does manifest.json need to be altered or something else? Please advice.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The `docker-archive` format simply doesn’t contain the relevant data to preserve the digest, and the data can’t be reconstructed. If you _have_ to use the `docker-archive` format (maybe because some scanner only supports that format), and you want to preserve original digests, I’m afraid you have no options at all.[1] (There’s another reason why this doesn’t work: `docker-archive` can not represent multi-architecture images, either.)

What I would generally recommend is a registry-to-registry copy to a temporary copy (e.g. running with a container), with the backend storage of that registry in a volume, and transferring that volume by tar. Alternatively, using the `dir:` transport, and transferring the individual directories by `tar`, would work (but potentially require more space because layers wouldn’t be shared across registries.

---

[1] A recent Docker update has started including OCI-formatted images inside `docker-archive` files. That is not currently supported by Skopeo, but anyway that would only work for images which are originally OCI, and that’s still fairly rare.

Thanks for your response, here is what I did

1. mkdir reg_data
2. docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --restart=always --name registry -v ~/reg_data:/var/lib/registry registry:2
3. skopeo copy docker://my-src-registry/my-repo-image:my-tag docker://localhost:5000/image:test
4. docker image ls --digests and it shows same digest for src_registry as well as localhost one -- good
5. tar -czvf my-image.tar.gz ~/reg_data/docker/registry/v2/repositories/my-image-name
6. skopeo copy tarball:my-image.tar.gz docker://destination-registr/repo-image:test

Now I would expect this to carry forward the digest to the destination registry like you said but it did not, could you please help me on what I need to correct here?

- `tarball:my-image.tar.gz` is not the same thing at all. You need to also run a registry in the destination (the ~same `docker run` command), and do a registry-to-registry copy.
- Using `skopeo copy --preserve-digests` (possibly with `--all`) might not make a difference right now, but it would add certainty: the result is either an exact copy or an error message.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
